title: cleomella palmeriana
intro: Cleomella palmeriana, also known as Palmer's cleome, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae), native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cleomella palmeriana include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
